description: 22-question SAT Math timed-test module — practice version 4. Mirrors the digital-SAT Module 1 structure: 22 questions, 35 minutes, Desmos calculator allowed.

answerFormat.MC: options is array of 4 strings. correct is 0-indexed integer.
answerFormat.SPR: correctAnswer is the canonical string answer. UI should normalize student input.
answerFormat.diagram: Optional SVG string. Frontend should render above question text when present.

questions.question: If 3(2x - 5) + 7 = 4(x + 1), what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 6x - 15 + 7 = 4x + 4. Simplify: 6x - 8 = 4x + 4. Subtract 4x: 2x - 8 = 4. Add 8: 2x = 12. Divide: x = 6.

questions.question: A florist sells roses for $3 each and a vase for $12. A customer's total order, including one vase, came to $33. How many roses did the customer buy?
If x + y = 12 and x - y = 4, what is the value of y?
questions.explanation: Add equations: 2x = 16, so x = 8. Then y = 12 - 8 = 4.

questions.question: A delivery service charges $5 for the first pound and $2 for each additional pound. If a customer wants to spend no more than $25 on shipping, what is the maximum weight, in pounds, of a single package they can ship?
questions.explanation: 5 + 2(w - 1) ≤ 25 → 3 + 2w ≤ 25 → 2w ≤ 22 → w ≤ 11. Max weight is 11 pounds.

questions.question: If x/3 + 5 = 11, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Subtract 5: x/3 = 6. Multiply by 3: x = 18.

questions.question: A line in the xy-plane passes through (-1, 4) and (3, -8). What is the slope of this line?
If y = 3x - 1 and 2x + y = 14, what is the value of x?
What is the larger of the two solutions to x² - 4x - 12 = 0?
questions.explanation: Factor: (x - 6)(x + 2) = 0. Solutions: x = 6 or x = -2. Larger is 6.

questions.question: A rectangular garden has a length that is 3 feet longer than its width. If the area of the garden is 40 square feet, what is the width, in feet?
questions.explanation: Let w = width. Then w(w + 3) = 40 → w² + 3w - 40 = 0 → (w + 8)(w - 5) = 0. Since width must be positive, w = 5 feet.

questions.question: A savings account starts with $2,000 and grows according to B(t) = 2000(1.04)^t, where B is the balance after t years. What does the value 1.04 represent in this context?
options: The growth factor: a 4% annual increase
The annual interest rate as a decimal
The total balance after one year
questions.explanation: In B(t) = a · r^t, the base r is the growth factor. r = 1.04 means a 4% increase per year.

questions.question: If f(x) = x + 5 and g(x) = 2x - 1, what is f(g(3))?
Which expression is equivalent to 3x(2x - 5) - 4(x - 1)?
If √(2x + 1) = 5, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Square both sides: 2x + 1 = 25. Subtract 1: 2x = 24. Divide: x = 12.

questions.question: For what value of x is the expression (x + 2)/(x - 7) undefined?
questions.explanation: A rational expression is undefined when its denominator equals 0. Set x - 7 = 0, so x = 7.

questions.question: A subscription that cost $60 last year now costs $72. What is the percent increase in cost?
questions.explanation: Increase = $72 - $60 = $12. Percent increase = 12/60 = 0.20 = 20%.

questions.question: On a map, 1 inch represents 50 miles. Two cities are 4.5 inches apart on the map. What is the actual distance between the cities, in miles?
questions.explanation: Actual distance = 4.5 × 50 = 225 miles.

questions.question: A standard deck of cards has 52 cards: 4 suits (hearts, diamonds, clubs, spades) with 13 cards each. If one card is drawn at random, what is the probability that it is a face card (Jack, Queen, or King)?
questions.explanation: Face cards: 3 per suit × 4 suits = 12. Probability = 12/52 = 3/13.

questions.question: The mean of 4 numbers is 12. Three of the numbers are 8, 10, and 15. What is the fourth number?
questions.explanation: Sum of 4 numbers = 4 × 12 = 48. Sum of 3 known = 8 + 10 + 15 = 33. Fourth number = 48 - 33 = 15.

questions.question: In a circle, an inscribed angle measures 35°. What is the measure, in degrees, of the central angle that intercepts the same arc?
questions.explanation: An inscribed angle is half the measure of the central angle that intercepts the same arc. So central angle = 2 × 35° = 70°.
questions.diagram:
<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <!-- Circle -->
  <circle cx="150" cy="150" r="100" fill="#FDF5EA" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Radii OA, OB (forming central angle) -->
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="92.6" y2="231.9" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="207.4" y2="231.9" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Chords CA, CB (forming inscribed angle) -->
  <line x1="150" y1="50" x2="92.6" y2="231.9" stroke="#C4602A" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="50" x2="207.4" y2="231.9" stroke="#C4602A" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Vertex dots -->
  <circle cx="150" cy="150" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <circle cx="92.6" cy="231.9" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <circle cx="207.4" cy="231.9" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>
  <circle cx="150" cy="50" r="3" fill="#2E2A25"/>

  <!-- Vertex labels -->
  <text x="78" y="250" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">A</text>
  <text x="215" y="250" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">B</text>
  <text x="143" y="44" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">C</text>
  <text x="158" y="146" font-family="Georgia, serif" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#2E2A25">O</text>

  <!-- Inscribed angle arc at C with 35° label (label positioned lower in the
       wider part of the angle so it doesn't crowd chord CA) -->
  <path d="M 141 79 A 14 14 0 0 0 159 79" fill="none" stroke="#C4602A" stroke-width="1.8"/>
  <text x="138" y="125" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#C4602A">35°</text>

  <!-- Central angle arc at O (unlabeled — that's the question) -->
  <path d="M 135.65 170.48 A 25 25 0 0 0 164.35 170.48" fill="none" stroke="#2E2A25" stroke-width="1.8"/>
</svg>

questions.question: In a right triangle whose acute angles measure 30° and 60°, the side opposite the 30° angle has length 5. What is the length of the side opposite the 60° angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle with acute angles 30° and 60°, the sides are in the ratio 1 : √3 : 2. The short leg (opposite 30°) is 5, so the longer leg (opposite 60°) is 5√3.

questions.question: What is the radius of the circle with equation (x - 2)² + (y + 1)² = 49?
questions.explanation: The standard form is (x - h)² + (y - k)² = r². Here r² = 49, so r = 7.

questions.question: In a right triangle, an acute angle has cosine equal to 3/5. What is the sine of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary, and sin(angle) = cos(complement). So sin of the other angle = cos of the first = 3/5.
